dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive - Real drug interaction database

pi_items.source: FDA approval records, Cancer Research UK, Mayo Clinic, MedlinePlus, NCI Dictionary. Ibrutinib (trade name IMBRUVICA) is FDA-approved for treating blood cancers including chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (CLL), mantle cell lymphoma (MCL), Waldenström's macroglobulinemia (WM), marginal zone lymphoma (MZL), and chronic graft versus host disease (cGVHD)

FDA labeling, Mayo Clinic, NHS, WebMD, MedlinePlus. Fluconazole is a triazole antifungal medication used to treat serious fungal or yeast infections, including candidiasis, oral thrush, and fungal meningitis. It is a CYP3A4 substrate inhibitor.

pi_items.metadata_value: Yes - there is a major interaction
pi_items.verified_value: Major interaction confirmed - Ibrutinib (CYP3A4 substrate) with Fluconazole (moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor)
pi_items.source: RxList, PDR, Drugs.com, American Journal of Therapeutics, FDA prescribing information. Fluconazole increases ibrutinib plasma concentrations approximately 3-fold. Clinical recommendation: reduce ibrutinib to 280 mg/day when coadministered with fluconazole.

pi_items.verified_value: Fluconazole (moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor) inhibits ibrutinib metabolism (CYP3A4 substrate), resulting in approximately 3-fold increase in ibrutinib AUC
pi_items.source: American Journal of Therapeutics (2020), PubMed article 32721144, FDA ClinPharmR documents, multiple drug interaction databases

Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app documentation and ibrutinib_comedication_contraindication_screen task README (https://github.com/liverpool-cancer/ichart-archive). The README explicitly states agents should navigate to 'the full Interaction Details screen' where drug names, severity indicators, and pharmacokinetic mechanisms are displayed.

pi_items.verified_value: Real Android application developed by Radboudumc (Nijmegen) and University of Liverpool Drug Interactions Group
pi_items.source: Cancer iChart on Google Play Store (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology), Cancer Drug Interactions website (https://cancer-druginteractions.org/), University of Liverpool announcement

pi_items.verified_value: The app uses the real Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database (Radboudumc and University of Liverpool collaborative project). Content last updated June 2022, based on published clinical literature and expert panel assessment.
pi_items.source: Cancer Drug Interactions website (https://cancer-druginteractions.org/), University of Liverpool Centre for Experimental Therapeutics

privileged_info_summary: Ibrutinib (Imbruvica) is a real FDA-approved Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor used for blood cancers including chronic lymphocytic leukaemia. Fluconazole is a real antifungal medication commonly used in cancer patients. A major drug interaction exists between them: fluconazole is a moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor that increases ibrutinib plasma concentrations approximately 3-fold, requiring dose reduction from standard 420 mg to 280 mg daily. This interaction is documented in the real Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive application, which displays interaction details including drug names, quality of evidence, severity ratings (traffic light system), and clinical descriptions on a dedicated 'Interaction Details' screen.
